Shqipëri e dashur,

që të kemi Nënë,

në duar të liga,

ke kohë që ke rënë!

Të liqtë e të liqve,

janë bashkuar prore,

të biesh në duar,

dhe më djallëzore!

Të biesh sërish,

në kthetrat e bishës,

në duart e Metës,

edhe të Berishës!

Sa kanë bërë këta,

kundër Shqipërisë,

s’kanë bërë gjithë armiqtë,

gjatë gjithë historisë!

Saliu s’e fsheh,

që është kriminel,

me Metën është ndryshe,

si shenjtor na del!

Për gjendjen ku jemi,

është kryesori,

një jetë të tërë,

veç vodhi dhe mori!

Këta që të gjithë,

s’dinë ku kanë paranë,

po barku u digjet,

keq për fukaranë!

Nga të gjitha anët,

ka shumë maskarenj,

fukarenjtë mbartin,

hallet si hamenj!

Si qentë-shpëtimtarët,

vetëm ham e hum,

s’di pse quajnë zgjedhjet,

një Referendum!

Të liq e kusarë,

pse s’u bie pika,

po presin në radhë,

Luli dhe Monika!

Shqipëri e dashur,

që të themi Nënë,

në ç’duar të zeza,

tridhjet vjet ke rënë!

Asnjë për t’u mburrur,

të gjithë për t’u sharë,

bir Selman i Nënës,

kë të qaj më parë!

Bir Selman i Nënës,

kë të qash më parë,

kanë vjedhur-kanë rrjepur,

po dhe kanë vrarë!

Kanë vjedhur e rrjepur,

po dhe kanë vrarë,

gjithë llumi i llumit,

sërish është në garë!

Si mund të votohet,

prapë kjo qënëri,

mos votoni njerëz,

rrini në shtëpi!

Nëse Drejtësia,

s’vihet në funksion,

këtu politika,

asnjëherë s’ndryshon!

Nëse Drejtësia,

me shpatë nuk pret,

të njëjtit palaço,

do jenë në pushtet!

Gjithnjë do t’ju hajë,

po i njëjti gjarpër,

dhe nesër do jini,

edhe më të varfër!

The irony of Asia being successful in controlling Covid-19 is…that Asia’s going to be later in getting to herd immunity

WSJ

After Crushing Covid-19 Last Year, These Countries Are Getting Left Behind in...

Asian countries led the world in crushing Covid last year, but now they are lagging behind on vaccinations and can’t afford to reopen borders, suggesting their economies may take longer to fully rebound.

China’s Covid vaccination programme beset by delay and reluctance

International travel restrictions expected until next year

While China has pledged to ship hundreds of millions of vaccines abroad, domestically the rollout of inoculations has been slow, raising concerns that restrictions on international travel will remain until at least next year. China was the first country to begin Covid-19 vaccinations in July and had administered 40.5m doses by early February, the highest number of injections behind the US. But at 2.9 doses per 100 residents, it is far behind other big economies and the levels needed to form herd immunity in the population. It has only given four-fifths of the vaccinations it had hoped to achieve by this point.
In some ways China’s vaccination programme has been a victim of the success of its containment policies. Rapid interventions last year brought the pandemic under control and saw case numbers tumble, reducing the incentive to get vaccinated. At the same time, the failure of vaccine manufacturers Sinopharm, Sinovac and CanSino to release comprehensive trial data has damaged confidence among some medical professionals, while many Chinese citizens are choosing to see first whether the vaccinations provoke unwanted side effects for others.

China’s slow vaccine rollout. Chart showing cumulative doses administered per 100 residents. Israel leads the world with 86 doses per 100 people as of Feb 24.
China has only managed 2.9 doses per 100 people as of Feb 9

“In China today there is very little upside for anyone to take the vaccine,” said Chen Long, partner at Beijing-based research company Plenum. “What that all means is a very slow opening of borders.” Chinese nationals who travel must quarantine for 14 days when they return, regardless of whether they have been vaccinated. Borders have been almost completely closed to foreign nationals since March 2020, with exceptions made only for some diplomats and a handful of business executives. Global pollster Ipsos found that 85 per cent of Chinese people intend to get vaccinated, putting them among the world’s most willing. But the study did not ask the participations when. A survey of mostly urban professionals by Beijing-based market research firm Gavekal Dragonomics found that of 307 respondents, some of whom had not yet been offered the jab, more than half did not plan to join the current round of vaccinations.

As in some other countries, including Germany and parts of the US, vaccine hesitancy in China appears particularly high among health professionals. A survey published in February by a Chinese medical journal showed that medical workers are unlikely to want vaccinations: out of 756 medical workers in Zhejiang province, only 28 per cent were ready to get jabs. For both the Gavekal and medical workers’ surveys, the most common reason to refrain from vaccination was fear of side-effects. Medical workers were also concerned about possible low efficacy, while both groups said they thought they did not need vaccination since the local risk of infection was so low. “Initially, we know so little about the vaccine, and there’s a lack of evidence to support its safety and efficacy. Plus, the situation in Beijing has been well under control for a while,” said a Beijing-based doctor who asked to remain anonymous. The doctor estimated that roughly a fifth of her hospital colleagues had not been vaccinated, though in many cases it was because their health status, such as taking other medication or attempting to get pregnant, had ruled them out.

Age restrictions on the use of the Sinopharm and Sinovac shots have also slowed the rollout. Both vaccines are recommended only for people in good health aged 18 to 59, which automatically excludes those who would most want a jab. Instead the government has focused on vaccinating those likely to spread the virus, such as taxi drivers. At this rate, analysts estimate it will take well into 2022 to vaccinate most of the population. Since January, Beijing’s city government has extended its vaccination campaign to more residential areas, sending texts to residents to encourage them to sign up. Compulsory vaccinations have been enforced by some state-owned enterprises. In some parts of Beijing, local urban management officers have asked bar staff either to get vaccinated or be tested every week. The municipal government has also made vaccination mandatory for cab drivers. Sinopharm and Sinovac together said they would have capacity to produce 2bn vaccines by the end of the year. Whether they achieve that target is uncertain: Sinovac is producing at a daily rate well below its stated capacity.

The manufacturers also face a shortage of glass vials to store vaccines, which are mostly imported. Supply is particularly constrained since the Sinopharm vaccine uses a single-dose vial. In contrast, each vial of BioNTech/Pfizer’s vaccine contains six doses, while a vial of Oxford/AstraZeneca’s vaccine contain 10 doses. Sinopharm and Sinovac also have to balance domestic demand with their global commitments. China’s foreign ministry has pledged 10m doses to the global vaccination programme Covax, which will mainly supply low-income countries. In addition, Chinese vaccine makers have pledged more than 500m doses in bilateral deals and delivered approximately 21m doses, according to Gavekal. But help is on the way. Before February 6, when Sinovac’s jab was approved, only a single vaccine developed by Sinopharm had been authorised for general use. Two additional candidates, one made by CanSino and a second shot from Sinopharm, were approved on Thursday bringing the total number of available vaccines to four.

Additional reporting by Nian Liu, Xueqiao Wang, and Christian Shepherd

Përballë nesh je ti, me psiqikën tënde të sëmurë që kujton se të sillesh si lac i lagjes mbulon paaftësinë tënde si kryeministër. Përballë nesh je vetëm ti që shite tokë e det e që arrite të përzësh këto 8 vite gjysëm milioni shqiptarë. Përballë nesh je vetëm ti, që shpërndave si në mall të babës tokë e det, porte dhe aeroporte për 5 oligarkë, ti që buxhetin e shqiptarëve e shkërrmoqe me klientët e tu ditën më të zezë të shqiptarve e kur kishin nevojë më shumë se kurrë për ndihmë. Përballë nesh je vetëm ti, që shkatërrove çdo shtyllë të sistemit demokratik, i le shqiptarët të vdesin mes pandemisë pa ndihmë. Ti dhe vetëm Ti, je përgjegjësi dhe kundërshtari ynë ndaj deri në fund do na kesh përballë, derisa ndëshkimi i drejtësisë së çliruar do të të çojë aty ku e meriton.

posts.name: Dafne
posts.cooked:
I was amazed by the pandemic but in my life I used to see the unexpected happen. Hitler’s rise was unexpected for the whole world. The German-Soviet Pact was unexpected and incredible. The resistance of Moscow has been incredible. The outbreak of the Algerian war was unexpected. I have only lived through the unexpected and habit of crises. In this sense, I’m experiencing a new crisis, huge, but which has all the characteristics of a crisis. That is, on the one hand, awakens creative imagination and, on the other hand, causes fear and mental regressions. And we all seek salvation, but we do not know how it will come.

We must learn to live with the unexpected, because in history the unexpected happens and will happen again. We thought we lived with false certainties, statistics, predictions, the idea that everything was stable when everything was already falling apart. We didn’t realize that. We must learn to live with uncertainty, i.e. have the courage to face and be willing to resist the negative forces that may come. So this is the question of changing the mindset. Today, for example, we forget that the biosphere crisis we learned about in the 70’s, is still getting worse everywhere. And here at this point, we should expect events we didn’t expect before.

Does judgement make us crazier or wiser? Both. A large part of people lose the bush while others become more lucid. The crisis favors the most contrasting forces. I hope it’s the creative forces, lucid forces and those who seek a new path, the ones that will dominate, even though they are still very scattered and weak. Otherwise, we will get lost in the anger that is justified, but that makes the mind blind and one sided.

We can rightfully be outraged, but we should not be blocked by frustration. We’ll have to see where we’re headed and what’s happening.

There’s something we forget: twenty years ago, a process started in which everything started to deteriorate in the world. The democracy crisis is not only in Latin America but also in European countries, threatening ourselves. The sovereignty of unlimited profit, which controls everything, exists in every country-and ours. So is the ecological crisis, not elsewhere, but here. So our minds will have to deal with the crises to overcome them. Otherwise, we will only be victims. We lived like sleepwalkers for ten years to end up with an awesome world war. I’m not saying that the circumstances are the same because Germany is not threatening us, but we see many conflicts emerge in the world. And we also see evidence of totalitarianism that has nothing to do with the last century.

We have all means of monitoring. Drones, cell phones, facial recognition. We have all the means to create a totalitarianism of surveillance. All the details are here. The problem is to prevent these elements from uniting and creating an unbearable society for us.

On the eve of my 100th birthday, what can I wish for? I wish strength, courage and clarity. We have to live in small oases of life and brotherhood.

Pak a shume​:joy: e njejta

Kete mund ta themi pas dates 25 prill. Pjese e rendesishme te asaj cfare mbetet eshte edhe menyra se si vdes.

Po ta mendosh mire, ajo qe ka rendesi nuk eshte se sa besonte (apo nuk besonte) tek nje virus i tille x apo y kryetar shteti, por se sa vlerë kishin masat shtrenguese (ose per te qene konkretë, locdown-i i mars/prill/majit) vjet, atje ku u aplikuan. Per shembull, në kete qoshken ku jam une (te themi rreth 10 miljone banore) asokohe nuk ka qarkulluar asnje kokerr virusi, por u mbyllem ‘se u mbyll gjithe bota’ (ose sepse “në Paris corona bente kerdinë”). Ndersa sot jemi te hapur (edhe pse mbahen mbyllur ‘me zor’ restorantet), nderkohe qe ketu tek une virusi qarkullon sh me teper se vjet ben mars !! Ose psh, studentet shqiptare kane Fiks nje vit qe nuk kane vene kemben ne nje auditor fakulteti. E keshtu me rradhe.
Problemi nuk eshte se kush eshte burre i mirë apo burre i keq nê kete histori (fundja te gjithe jemi njerez prej mishi dhe gjaku) por ç’rezultat efektiv jep x apo y masë dhe si qendron peshorja midis efekt/pasoje (por me sa kuptoj, pasojat do jene diku midis afat-mesme e afat-gjata : per shembull, në France tashme ka dalë moda qe borxhin publik i cili ka kaluar nga 89% e GDP në shkurt 2020 në 125% e GDP në shkurt te 2021 (pra vecse per nje vit +35% !!) do e paguajne gjeneratat e ardhshme. Dhe kjo thuhet me qetesine më te madhe ! (biles gati gati me nje far krenarie)

Sa per zgjedhjet shqiptare te 25 prillit : djegia e mandateve nga ana e PD para dy vjetesh eshte garancia absolute per PS qe ti rifitoje zgjedhjet (ça t’thush, me fat Rama, seriozisht !!).
Më 26 prill, te nesermen e zgjedhjeve, PD do jete fiks atje ku ish në 26 qershor te 2017, pra te nesermen e zgjedhjeve parlamentare te para 4 viteve. Dmth në opozite.

Mirë në opozite, por në opozitë… nga e para ! (te kish ndenjur në parlament, sot, me krizen e virusit etj etj, opozita do ti kish 50-50 shancet per te fituar; tani i ka zero).

Ajo qe beson a nuk beson nje kryetar shteti mund te kete rendesi (per shembull fakti qe Brazili eshte ne kete gjendje ku eshte thone qe vjen edhe nga fakti qe Bolsanaro a cfare e ka ai, “nuk beson te virusi”), po kjo e ketij te Tanzanise paska qene pune me e thelle, fetare, wudu a ku e di une se cfare, shto edhe faktin qe “nuk i ben nder” e gjithe kesaj fshehtesie, nuk i raporton shifrat OBSh-se e me radhe (jo se keta te OBSh-se jane burra te mire, plehra politike a biopolitike sic mund t’i quaje ose mund te mos i quaje ti ose Fukoja). Problemi nuk eshte pse ndonje qoshke si ajo kur je ti u mbyll kur nuk kishte kokerr virusi dhe u hap kur nuk kishe ku te hidhje kokrren e molles nga virusi, flasim ne parim. Sic me ra goja duke e thene, bilanci behet ne fund, eshte heret per ta bere.
